{{Quote|She was still impressive, wasn't she… even all mangled and broken like that. My ''Anya Karu'' was a beautiful ship.|Captain Aron Harcourt, to Boba Fett|<ref name="Wreck" />}}
The '''''Anya Karu''''' was an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] serving the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during or prior to [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]], shortly before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Helmed by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Aron Harcourt]], the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] was brought down by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Saboteur/Legends|saboteurs]] and left broken on a [[Unidentified planet (Anya Karu)|desolate planet]]. Disgraced and demoted by the ''Anya Karu''{{'s}} demise, Harcourt hired the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to retrieve a [[Holorecorder/Legends|holorecording]] of his [[Death/Legends|departed]] [[Janelle Harcourt|wife]] from the wreckage of the ''Anya Karu''. Fett was able to retrieve the holorecording moments before the Imperial [[Liberator (Imperial I-class)|''Adjudicator'']] destroyed the ''Anya Karu''{{'s}} fuselage.

==Description==
As an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], the ''Anya Karu''<ref name="Wreck" /> was manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]], one of the largest [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. At 1,600 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length,<ref name="Warfare" /> the ''Anya Karu'' possessed the same characteristics of other [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]], such as the massive [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] located toward the aft of the [[Warship/Legends|warship]].<ref name="Wreck" />

==History==
{{Quote|They ended my career… rather than admit they had lost one of their capital ships… to a team of Rebel Saboteurs.|Captain Aron Harcourt, discussing the events after the loss of the ''Anya Karu''|<ref name="Wreck" />}}
[[File:SWLEC-TheRebellionVol3-CoverArt.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Boba Fett in the ''Anya Karu'']]
Helmed by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Aron Harcourt]], the ''Anya Karu'' belonged to the Galactic Empire's [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during or prior to [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="Wreck" /> shortly before the outbreak of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Source">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> As Harcourt was a hero of the Empire, he and the ''Anya Karu'' were granted certain privileges, one such being that Harcourt could keep an [[RHTC-560 Hunter Trainer]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and two [[RHTC-560 HT Drone]]s as [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], given to him by a [[Unidentified Rodian warlord|Rodian]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]].<ref name="Wreck" /> Sometime before or during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] 3 BBY, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agents [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaged]] the ''Anya Karu'', causing it to crash<ref name="Wreck" /><ref name="CSWE" /> on a [[Unidentified planet (Anya Karu)|barren planet]]. As the vessel went down, Harcourt gave the order to abandon ship.<ref name="Wreck" />

In response to the loss of one of their [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]], the Imperials put in a formal [[Inquiry on the sabotaging of the Anya Karu|Inquiry]] before having Harcourt used as the scapegoat for the incident,<ref name="Wreck" /> forcing him out of the command structure and placing him in control of a distant outpost,<ref name="CSWE" /> as the Empire did not want to admit that they had lost one of their [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to Rebel sabotage. Around three years after the incident, Harcourt hired the infamous [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to retrieve an old [[Holorecorder/Legends|holorecording]] that he had left on the ''Anya Karu'' of his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Janelle Harcourt]], who had [[Death/Legends|died]] around 3 BBY.<ref name="Wreck" />

[[File:Anya_Karu_destroyed.png|thumb|right|180px|''Slave I'' escapes as the ''Anya Karu'' is destroyed]]
Fett arrived at the wreckage of the ''Anya Karu'' in his [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|''Firespray-31''-class patrol and attack craft]] [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']], destroyed the two [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] patrolling the restricted area, and boarded the ship twenty-five [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]] to spare before the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Liberator (Imperial I-class)|''Adjudicator'']] arrived to destroy it. While onboard the ''Anya Karu'', the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter discovered a dead [[Unidentified stormtrooper (Anya Karu)|stormtrooper]], fought off a pack of winged [[Mynock/Legends|mynocks]] that had taken refuge on the ship, and destroyed Harcourt's old [[Hunting/Legends|Hunter]] Trainer [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. Fett secured the recording and left the ship, which was destroyed via [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire from the ''Adjudicator'' [[Standard second|seconds]] later.<ref name="Wreck" />

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Anya Karu'' was under the command of Captain Aron Harcourt, with thirty years of experience as a commanding [[Officer/Legends|officer]] by the time she was sabotaged. Serving as bodyguards to Harcourt were an RHTC-560 Hunter Trainer and two RHTC-560 HT drones, gifted to him from a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] warlord. Also in service aboard the ''Anya Karu'' was at least one Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]], whose body Fett later found while searching the ship.<ref name="Wreck" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Anya Karu'' first appeared [[Empire 28|''Empire'' 28]], a [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] illustrated by [[Adriana Melo]], [[Michael Atiyeh]], and [[Michael David Thomas]], and scripted by [[Ron Marz]]. The issue was released by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="Wreck" /> on [[December 29]], [[2004]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54198/star_wars_empire_2002_28|text=Star Wars: Empire (2002) #28}}</ref>
